title: Qoro Launches Solo: A Cloud Platform for Parallelized Quantum Simulation - Quantum Computing Report
why:
- Qoro Quantum has launched Solo, a self-serve cloud platform designed to accelerate hybrid quantum-classical workload simulations by using multilayered parallelization instead of traditional sequential queuing.
- In internal benchmarks, Solo completed a 9,000-job QAOA max-cut workload in under 10 minutes, compared to over 24 hours on conventional setups.
- The Solo platform serves as an access layer for an integrated software stack (including Divi, Composer, and Maestro) that treats CPUs, GPUs, and QPUs as a single logical machine, enabling users to switch compute modalities without rewriting code.
- The launch aims to lower the barrier to entry for quantum development and is targeted at large-scale problems in finance, logistics, and materials science that exceed the capability of classical servers but are too complex for current quantum processors.
